meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No-Code Barriers
content.seo_description: Overcome no-coding experience with a professional SaaS starter kit. Learn to scale your freelance business using Antigravity and Claude Code in this guide.
content.answer_block: To overcome the no-coding barrier, leverage a production-ready SaaS starter kit. By combining Antigravity for automated boilerplate generation and Claude Code for iterative logic implementation, you can ship professional, database-driven applications in days rather than weeks, effectively bypassing the need for deep traditional software engineering expertise.
content.intro:
The freelance landscape is shifting. Clients no longer pay for 'ideas'; they pay for high-velocity execution. When you hit the wall of 'no coding experience,' you aren't just losing a project—you're losing your competitive edge in a market that demands SaaS-level output at a freelancer's speed.

But the era of manual boilerplate construction is over. By treating your development stack as a modular, repeatable system, you can transform from a non-coder into a high-leverage AI Builder. We’re going to bridge that gap using the Antigravity architecture and the surgical precision of Claude Code to ship your first SaaS project.

steps.title: Architecting the Stack with Antigravity
steps.description: Define your schema and project requirements to generate a clean, modular foundation without writing a single line of boilerplate code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack SaaS starter kit structure using Next.js 14, Supabase for authentication and database management, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure the architecture includes protected routes and a modular API folder structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize project directory with Antigravity output and run npm install'

steps.title: Implementing Authentication Flow
steps.description: Secure your application by integrating Supabase Auth, ensuring your users can sign up and sign in seamlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure middleware.ts file and a login/signup component that handles Supabase session persistence and redirects unauthenticated users to the landing page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure Supabase client and implement protected route middleware'

steps.title: Database Schema Definition
steps.description: Map your application data using SQL or schema definitions to ensure your starter kit is ready for production scaling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Supabase migration script for a 'profiles' table and a 'projects' table, ensuring row-level security (RLS) is enabled and enforced.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Run database migration and verify table schema against project requirements'

steps.title: Building the Core Feature Set
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on business logic, turning your specific app requirements into functional React components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard component that fetches data from the 'projects' table and displays it in a responsive data grid using Shadcn UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement Dashboard UI with data fetching logic and error handling'

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Finalize your build and deploy to Vercel to ensure your app is accessible globally with automated production updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a vercel.json configuration and a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs linting and build checks on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure deployment pipeline and verify production build'

pro_tips: Always define your database schema as a single source of truth before generating components.
Use Claude Code's 'review' mode to audit generated code for security vulnerabilities before pushing to production.
Stick to a single UI library like Shadcn to ensure your Antigravity components remain consistent.
Implement feature flags early to ship partial features safely without breaking the production build.
Focus your AI prompts on 'functional requirements' rather than 'styling' to keep the codebase clean and maintainable.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, focus on adding a payment gateway like Stripe and setting up automated email notifications to turn your project into a revenue-generating asset.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical barriers dictate your freelance income. By mastering these tools, you move from a designer to a builder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
